heading: Transfer and administration of lands
label: 410aaa–23 Transfer and administration of lands
text: On , the Secretary shall transfer the lands under the jurisdiction of the Bureau of Land Management depicted on the maps described in , without consideration, to the administrative jurisdiction of the National Park Service for administration as part of the National Park System. The boundaries of the park shall be adjusted accordingly. The Secretary shall administer the areas added to the park by this part in accordance with the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including the Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; , 2–4).October 31, 1994August 25, 1916section 410aaa–22 of this title39 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 11See References in Text note below.1
notes: References in TextThe Act entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes”, approved (; , 2–4), referred to in text, is , , known as the National Park Service Organic Act, which enacted sections 1, 2, 3, and 4 of this title and provisions set out as a note under , National Park Service and Related Programs. Sections 1 to 4 of the Act were repealed and restated as , Crimes and Criminal Procedure, and section 100101(a), chapter 1003, and sections 100751(a), 100752, 100753, and 102101 of Title 54 by , §§ 3, 4(a)(1), 7, , , 3260, 3272. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.
